rulings.Case Title: JOHN DOBROCKE et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 14, 2010, line 19, DEFENDANT METALCLAD INSULATION CORPORATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for Summary Judgment withdrawn. Motion for summary adjudication granted in part and denied in part. Motion denied as to 1st cause of action for negligence. Motion granted as to 2nd cause of action for strict liability, 3rd cause of action for false representation, 4th cause of action for loss of consortium, 5th cause of action for premises liability, and punitive damages request. Motion conceded as to false representation. No triable issue of fact regarding strict liability, no evidence that defendant sold or supplied asbestos containing products that defendant was exposed to. No evidence that defendant controlled the premises or supervised plaintiff. Loss of consortium barred due to fact wife was not married to plaintiff at time of exposure to asbestos containing products. Zwicker vs. Altamont Emergency Room (2002) 98 Cal.App. 4th 26 and its analysis are controlling. Although a delayed discovery rule can toll a cause of action, thereby preventing a tort claim from expiring before it is discovered, the delayed discovery rule has no place in determining whether a tort claim ever arose in the first place. (See Zwicker at page 34.) The loss of consortium claim is not "parasitic" upon the physically injuried spouse's cause of action. (See Uram vs. Abex Corp. (1990) 217 Cal.App.3rd 1425.) The discovery rule cannot create a cause of action where one does not exist. Court does not rule on transcripts from prior cases in view of their insufficiency to raise a triable issue of fact regarding punitive damages. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND

Public policy favors hearing the case on the merits. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: FEED BAG, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. LILLIE A MOSADDEGH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 14, 2010, line 17, PLAINTIFF FEED BAG, INC., MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion granted. Plaintiff shifts the burden and no triable issue of material fact raised. No evidence presented that the defendant or Dr. Matthews gave the required written notice to exercise the second option. No dispute regarding damges that are based on a figure found in defendant's discovery responses. =(302/CWW)
